Run health checks on the project (bun, config, theme, content, network)

Usage:
  laurel doctor [--config <path>] [--json] [--network] [--no-network]

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -j, --json          Emit results as JSON (for CI consumption)
  --network           Run the network reachability check
  --no-network        Skip the network reachability check
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_DOCTOR_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel doctor                                # full project health check
  laurel doctor --no-network                   # skip the connectivity probe
  laurel doctor --json                         # machine-readable for CI
